dashboard.label: T1090.003 — Multi-hop Proxy / Tor
dashboard.description: Detects Tor client activity by counting distinct relay-IP connections per source over a 1-hour window. Lookup-driven (app/lookups/tor_relays.csv); refresh from torbulkexitlist in production.

search.query: `tor_relay_use` | where like(src, "$src_filter$") OR "$src_filter$"="*" | stats sum(distinct_relays) AS relays

`tor_relay_use` | where like(src, "$src_filter$") OR "$src_filter$"="*" | eval window=strftime(_time, "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M") | sort -distinct_relays | table window, src, distinct_relays, total_connections, sample_relays, relay_types, duration_seconds

panel.title: Connections to relay set, by source

search.query: index=zeek sourcetype=zeek:conn | lookup tor_relays.csv ip AS "id.resp_h" OUTPUT relay_type | where isnotnull(relay_type) | timechart span=1h count BY src limit=10

html.p: Tor clients route victim traffic through three relay hops chosen from the public consensus, rotating entry guards over the long term and rebuilding circuits every ~10 minutes. Over an hour a single Tor client touches several distinct relay IPs — vanilla browsing touches none.

The detection enriches conn.log with the tor_relays.csv lookup and flags any source touching ≥ 3 distinct relay IPs in a 1-hour window. The lab ships synthetic relay IPs so tests are hermetic; in production replace the lookup with a feed from torbulkexitlist on a cron.
